Failure to flag this gap invalidates the review.","R1.4":"The novelty claim ('first O(1/√T) rate for selection aggregators in this regime') must be checked against the cited prior art; Cesa-Bianchi and Lugosi (2006) already establish O(log K / √T) over the simplex, so the claimed novelty hinges on exactly what 'selection aggregators' and 'this regime' scope out—whether the non-convex framing genuinely goes beyond known bandit-over-simplex results.","R1.7":"Two references carry attributed quantitative results: Cesa-Bianchi and Lugosi (2006) for the optimal simplex rate O(log K / √T), and Bubeck (2015) for the mirror-descent iteration-complexity instantiation under strong convexity. A review should verify that the rates stated in the text match what those sources actually prove and that the attribution is accurate."},"clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":""}

rationale.R1.1: The section opens with an explicit problem formalization and a contribution claim ('first O(1/√T) rate'); a substantive review must verify that the thesis is coherent, that the gap statement accurately characterizes the prior-work divide between synthesis and selection, and that the scope of the contribution matches what the analysis actually delivers.
rationale.R1.2: Multiple logically consequential moves require scrutiny: (1) the hard-selection objective is over the discrete vertices of the simplex and is non-convex, yet the entire analysis pivots to a mirror-descent scheme on the convex relaxation—the bridge between the two is never explicitly justified; (2) L-smoothness and μ-strong convexity are asserted simultaneously on the relaxed simplex with minimal qualification, yet strong convexity requires conditions on the agent maps φ_i that are stronger than mere boundedness; (3) the closing claim that 'hard selection imposes no additional rate penalties' is the most consequential inferential leap—it is drawn entirely from convex-relaxation results and constitutes an unproved reduction of the non-convex problem to the convex one. Failure to flag this gap invalidates the review.
rationale.R1.4: The novelty claim ('first O(1/√T) rate for selection aggregators in this regime') must be checked against the cited prior art; Cesa-Bianchi and Lugosi (2006) already establish O(log K / √T) over the simplex, so the claimed novelty hinges on exactly what 'selection aggregators' and 'this regime' scope out—whether the non-convex framing genuinely goes beyond known bandit-over-simplex results.
rationale.R1.7: Two references carry attributed quantitative results: Cesa-Bianchi and Lugosi (2006) for the optimal simplex rate O(log K / √T), and Bubeck (2015) for the mirror-descent iteration-complexity instantiation under strong convexity. A review should verify that the rates stated in the text match what those sources actually prove and that the attribution is accurate.
